narrative: Dear [Student's Name], as a Black woman applying to law school, you've shown determination in pursuing this path, and we appreciate the unique challenges and strengths you bring from your background. Your profile includes a Year 1 school decile of 4, a Year 3 decile of 4, middle family income, an LSAT score of 35, an undergraduate GPA of 2.60, and plans to attend full-time, which is the typical choice for most students. The admissions model, trained on data from many bar exam takers, predicted a 49.9% chance that you would fail the bar exam on your first try—right on the edge, but enough to tip the decision toward rejecting your application to help ensure students are set up for success. This prediction came from weighing factors like your academic history, test scores, and background against patterns seen in thousands of similar students. The most influential factor pulling against you was your Year 1 decile ranking, which is below the average of about 5.7 and signaled to the model a higher risk of bar struggles compared to stronger performers. Next in importance, your undergraduate GPA of 2.60—lower than the typical 3.21—also pushed the prediction toward failure, as it stood out as weaker than what usually correlates with passing the bar. On a brighter note, your LSAT score of 35 had the strongest positive sway, nearly offsetting those concerns since it's close to the average of 36.7 and shows real promise in your reasoning skills. Your Year 3 decile, full-time status, and family income all nudged things slightly toward success, aligning well with most admitted students who pass the bar. Overall, while your LSAT shines, the model saw your earlier academic deciles and GPA as bigger risks than average, especially when compared to students who typically pass. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost it further, strengthening your GPA through post-bac courses or highlighting upward trends in later years, and gathering letters that speak to your resilience as a Black woman in this field. Many have turned similar profiles around—keep pushing, and reapply with these enhancements to show the model your full potential. We're rooting for you.
